call_title: 1st Open Call under RDI Fund through TDB as Second-Level Fund Manager
applicant_layer_reason: Eligible Indian technology entities, including DPIIT-recognised startups at TRL 4 and above, apply through TDB's RDIF registration route.
parent_resolution_reason: RDIF is the permanent fund; TDB is its designated Second-Level Fund Manager for this application route.
status_reason: Open status is supported by the active official Apply Now route and operational RDIF registration form; no closing date is published.
startup_relevance_reason: The official TDB page explicitly includes DPIIT-defined startups among eligible technology entities receiving RDIF support through SLFMs.
secondary_sectors: ClimateTech & Clean Energy; Artificial Intelligence & Data; Biotechnology & Medical Technologies; Digital Economy & AgriTech; Space Technology; Semiconductors & Electronics
sector_reason: The official RDIF/TDB pages enumerate sunrise and strategic sectors including clean energy, quantum, robotics, space, AI, agriculture, health, biotechnology, medical devices and the digital economy.
eligible_applicants: Indian legal entities including DPIIT-defined startups, companies, partnerships and LLPs; resident-Indian control, Indian headquarters and RDI-intensive technology at TRL 4 or above are required
funding_summary: Long-tenure collateral-free loan, equity or hybrid support; normally up to 50% of assessed project cost. No grant funding. TDB states 3%–4% interest and a 12–15 year period for RDIF loans.
evidence_note: Curated from official DST, RDIF/ANRF and TDB sources on 2026-07-11.
status_evidence: The official TDB RDIF-SLFM page displays an active Apply Now link to the RDIF registration portal; the TDB page was last updated on 08/07/2026.
